## Description

<!-- What does this PR do? Why is it needed? Link issues/specs: Fixes #123, Relates to #456 -->

## Type of Change

<!-- Check all that apply -->

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] Performance improvement
- [ ] Refactor (no functional change)
- [ ] Build / CI / tooling change
- [ ] Security fix

## Testing

<!-- How was this change verified? -->

- [ ] Unit tests added / updated
- [ ] Integration tests added / updated
- [ ] Manual smoke test performed
- [ ] Linter / formatter run (e.g. `cargo fmt --check`, `clippy`, `eslint`)
- [ ] CI is green on this branch

## Checklist

<!-- Confirm each item before requesting review -->

- [ ] Commit message follows [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) (e.g. `feat(scope): ...`, `fix: ...`, `chore: ...`)
- [ ] **Breaking change?** If yes, a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer is present in the commit body and migration notes are included in this PR description
- [ ] Tests added for new behavior (or rationale provided for why none are needed)
- [ ] Documentation updated (README, CHANGELOG, AGENTS.md, inline rustdoc/docstrings, etc.)
- [ ] Self-review of diff completed
- [ ] No new warnings introduced (`cargo build`, `clippy`, language-specific lint passes)
- [ ] Branch is up to date with the base branch (rebased / merged)
